quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592. He claimed to have found the passage between the Pacific Ocean and the Atlantic, though historians now believe he may have been describing the strait that bears his name today.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny birds have hearts that beat up to 1,260 times per minute and can remember every flower they've visited. Learn about the amazing abilities of these jewel-like creatures that visit gardens all summer long.

medium_read.title: The Ancient Forests of the Olympic Peninsula
medium_read.teaser: Olympic National Park is home to some of the largest temperate rainforests in the United States, with trees over 1,000 years old. This beautiful exploration takes you through the moss-draped valleys and towering giants right in your backyard.

long_read.title: The Gardens of Hungarian Castles
long_read.teaser: From the terraced gardens of Buda Castle to the romantic landscapes of Esterházy Palace, Hungary has a rich tradition of beautiful garden design. This journey through history explores how Hungarian gardens blended Eastern and Western influences into something uniquely their own.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season
port_angeles.description: Late June is the height of harbor seal pupping season in the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Mother seals and their spotted pups can often be seen resting on the rocks near Ediz Hook and along the waterfront. If you spot them, remember to keep a respectful distance so mothers and babies can bond safely.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's wings can beat approximately ___ times per second.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That's incredibly fast — so fast our eyes just see a blur!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! Hummingbird wings beat about 50 times per second — no wonder they hum!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Hummingbird wings actually beat about 50 times per second — that's why they make that distinctive humming sound!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: This rapid wing movement allows hummingbirds to hover in place and even fly backwards, making them the only birds with this special ability.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's perfect — you know your Hungarian!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'Köszönöm' — a beautiful word you probably use often! (Szia means 'hi', Viszlát means 'goodbye', and Igen means 'yes'.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European languages that surround it. Its closest relatives are Finnish and Estonian.

puzzle_3.question: What type of trees are most common in Olympic National Park's temperate rainforests?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Sitka spruce and western hemlock create those magical, moss-covered forests we love here on the peninsula.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually Sitka spruce and western hemlock! These giants thrive in our wet climate and create those beautiful green cathedral forests.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh Rain Forest and Quinault Rain Forest in Olympic National Park receive over 12 feet of rain per year, creating perfect conditions for these enormous trees and the thick carpet of moss that covers everything.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Visit the Hoh Rain Forest — National Park Service

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
